source.title: Crawl endpoint · Cloudflare Browser Rendering docs

summary: The /crawl endpoint starts an asynchronous crawl job for a seed URL and returns a job that can be polled until completed. It is for multi-page content acquisition, not a one-shot page fetch.

purpose: Use /crawl when the task is multi-page content acquisition rather than a single rendered page.
The endpoint is documented as Beta in the Browser Rendering navigation.

requestNotes: The captured API example shows a minimal body with only a seed url.
Use Cloudflare account auth exactly as documented in the official examples.
jobLifecycle: Create a crawl job with POST /crawl.
Delete the job with DELETE /crawl/<jobId> when cleanup is needed.

observedLocalArtifact.certaintyNote: These fields are observed in the repo-local successful crawl artifact. Treat them as strong evidence, not a guaranteed full contract unless the docs say so.

practicalGuidance: Repo-local successful crawl output confirms completed job records contain cleaned markdown per crawled page plus status/metadata.
The real local artifact also shows heavy skip counts, so result review matters more than assuming broad coverage.

cleaning.notes: This is a concise tracked summary. It separates the documented request flow from fields observed in the repo-local artifact so certainty boundaries stay honest.
